2442.reason: [Error: When the right parenthesis is missing, this method calls getCharWithJudge with RPARENT, which should report error 'j' on the line of the last non-terminal before the missing ')'. However, the current implementation in getCharWithJudge uses tokens.get(index-1).lineIndex, which may be incorrect when the missing ')' occurs after a function parameter list that ends with an error (e.g., missing ']' or other issues). In the test case, for line 1 "void f(int a {}", the function definition has a missing ')' after the parameter, but the parser incorrectly reports error 'j' on line 1 (which is correct) but then fails to report error 'j' on line 3 for "f(a + 10;" because the parsing of the function call in parseStmt does not properly handle the missing ')' and instead reports error 'i' (missing semicolon) on line 5. The root cause is that in parseStmt, when parsing a function call (IDENFR followed by LPARENT), the code does not call getCharWithJudge for RPARENT, but instead manually calls tokenList.getChar() after parsing FuncRParams, which does not trigger the error reporting for missing ')'.]

However, the current implementation in getCharWithJudge uses tokens.get(index-1).lineIndex, which may be incorrect when the missing ')' occurs after a function parameter list that ends with an error (e.g., missing ']' or other issues). In the test case, for line 1 "void f(int a {}", the function definition has a missing ')' after the parameter, but the parser incorrectly reports error 'j' on line 1 (which is correct) but then fails to report error 'j' on line 3 for "f(a + 10;" because the parsing of the function call in parseStmt does not properly handle the missing ')' and instead reports error 'i' (missing semicolon) on line 5. The root cause is that in parseStmt, when parsing a function call (IDENFR followed by LPARENT), the code does not call getCharWithJudge for RPARENT, but instead manually calls tokenList.getChar() after parsing FuncRParams, which does not trigger the error reporting for missing ')'.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: When the right parenthesis is missing, getCharWithJudge does not report error 'j' because it only checks for RPARENT type and returns null without adding error; the error should be reported on the line of the last non-terminal before the missing ')', which is the line of the function parameter or '(']

2906.reason: [Error: This line calls tokenList.getChar() instead of tokenList.getCharWithJudge(TokenType.RPARENT). When the right parenthesis is missing, this does not report error 'j'. The expected behavior is to report error 'j' on the line of the last non-terminal before the missing ')', which in this case is the line of the function call expression. In the test case, for line 3 "f(a + 10;", the function call "f(a + 10" is missing a ')', but the parser does not report error 'j' on line 3; instead, it later reports error 'i' on line 5 because the missing ')' causes the parser to consume the ';' as part of the expression, leading to a missing semicolon error later.]

571.reason: [When multiple token types are expected (types.length > 1), no error is reported. In the test case line 5 "f(a + 10;", the parser expects RPARENT but encounters SEMICN. Since the method is called with multiple expected types in some contexts, it fails to report error "j" and instead later reports error "i" when SEMICN is expected but not found.]

2646.reason: The code blindly consumes the next token instead of verifying it is a right parenthesis ')'. This causes missing ')' errors to be ignored here and potentially misreported as missing semicolons later. It should use tokenList.getCharWithJudge(TokenType.RPARENT).

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The code blindly consumes the next token instead of verifying it is a right parenthesis ')'. This causes missing ')' errors to be ignored here and potentially misreported as missing semicolons later. It should use tokenList.getCharWithJudge(TokenType.RPARENT).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  This line blindly consumes the next token (expected to be ')') without checking if it is actually ')'. If ')' is missing, it consumes the subsequent token (e.g., ';'), causing the parser to miss the syntax error here and potentially report a wrong error (like missing semicolon) later. It should use tokenList.getCharWithJudge(TokenType.RPARENT) to correctly detect and report error 'j'.
